extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AVERMAET Greg attacked final 500m on summit finish circuit, held off SAGAN's sprint by 2 seconds to claim race lead
tactical_patterns: Tinkoff put two riders (Gavia, Bennati) in breakaway supporting Sagan, revealing world champion's GC ambitions beyond sprint stages.
Eight-rider split formed on circuit's finishing ramp with 16-second gap, destroying Movistar's numerical advantage before sprint.
Van Avermaet attacked inside final 500m rather than opening sprint, exploiting Sagan's hesitation to follow early moves.
Valverde's Movistar led chase despite no team interest in outcome, suggesting defensive riding to protect others' GC positions.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Lost despite numerical advantage and lead-out, hesitated when Van Avermaet attacked at 500m suggesting punch-finishing weakness
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Timed attack perfectly inside 500m knowing Sagan wouldn't respond early, tactical maturity beyond typical puncheur sprinting
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: Lost 4 seconds in finale despite Sunweb numbers, revealing vulnerability on short explosive climbs even at low gradient
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Dropped from top-10 GC entirely on short circuit finish, exposing poor form despite pre-race leadership
